channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: These AI models are free, private, and will never say 'no'
item.description: Open-weight AI models with advanced capabilities and no safeguards are becoming much more accessible. While they can be useful, AI safety experts have concerns.
item.content:encoded:
Open-weight AI models with advanced capabilities and no safeguards are becoming much more accessible. While they can be useful, AI safety experts have concerns.

(Image credit: Patrick Lux)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: DOJ charges Google staffer over Polymarket trades netting $1.2 million
item.description: It's the second known case of the federal government filing criminal charges against someone who allegedly used insider information to make a large profit on a prediction market site.
item.content:encoded:
It's the second known case of the federal government filing criminal charges against someone who allegedly used insider information to make a large profit on a prediction market site.

(Image credit: Josh Edelson)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: He filmed himself doing household tasks — for AI robots
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Reece Rogers of WIRED about a new wave of data collection marketplaces, where users can sell their videos of everyday tasks to AI developers.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Reece Rogers of WIRED about a new wave of data collection marketplaces, where users can sell their videos of everyday tasks to AI developers.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow

item.title: Researchers are building AI-powered robot labs. What does this mean for science?
item.description: Thanks to new technologies like artificial intelligence, scientists are increasingly freed from the constraints of the laboratory. It raises questions about how much humans should outsource to robots.
item.content:encoded: Thanks to new technologies like artificial intelligence, scientists are increasingly freed from the constraints of the laboratory. It raises questions about how much humans should outsource to robots.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: This big university system is embracing AI. Students and faculty aren't all on board
item.description: The California State University system offers an early look at what happens when an administration commits to a technology that its own community isn't convinced will improve education.
item.content:encoded: The California State University system offers an early look at what happens when an administration commits to a technology that its own community isn't convinced will improve education.
item.dc:creator: Lee V. Gaines

item.title: DHS says ICE has 'no relationship' with spyware maker Paragon Solutions
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security says ICE has no current contract or relationship with the company, but questions remain about ICE's use of commercial spyware.
item.content:encoded:
The Department of Homeland Security says ICE has no current contract or relationship with the company, but questions remain about ICE's use of commercial spyware.

(Image credit: Timothy A. Clary)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: Trump cancels AI executive order signing
item.description: President Trump struggles to make a plan for artificial intelligence regulation after initially taking a hands off position on the emerging technology.
item.content:encoded: President Trump struggles to make a plan for artificial intelligence regulation after initially taking a hands off position on the emerging technology.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Ask AI or just Google it? Google makes a big change to a little search box
item.description: The search giant is updating its famously minimalist homepage. But what looks like a tiny design change is a very big deal.
item.content:encoded:
The search giant is updating its famously minimalist homepage. But what looks like a tiny design change is a very big deal.

(Image credit: GLENN CHAPMAN/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: A trillion dollar question: Will SpaceX's Starship launch go well?
item.description: Ahead of a much anticipated IPO, SpaceX is carrying out a critical test of its giant, stainless steel rocket. Investors will be watching closely.
item.content:encoded:
Ahead of a much anticipated IPO, SpaceX is carrying out a critical test of its giant, stainless steel rocket. Investors will be watching closely.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Advice for 2026 commencement speakers: Don't bring up AI
item.description: Commencement speakers who bring up the sweeping changes that artificial intelligence is driving are facing boos from the Class of 2026.
item.content:encoded:
Commencement speakers who bring up the sweeping changes that artificial intelligence is driving are facing boos from the Class of 2026.

(Image credit: University of Central Florida and Middle Tennessee State University via Storyful)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block
